info.name: VidyaPrayag API (backend-by-abuzar)
info.description:
End-to-end Postman collection for the VidyaPrayag Ktor backend.

This collection assumes:
• You're running the server on `http://localhost:8080` (see env baseUrl).
• OTP_DEV_RETURN_CODE=true in your local `.env` — so /send-otp echoes back a `dev_code` field that the test scripts auto-extract into `{{dev_otp}}`.
• You have set the `phone_number` env var to a real Indian mobile in E.164 (`+919XXXXXXXXX`). The server normalises 10-digit input to +91… too.

Recommended test order (top to bottom):
  0. Liveness → 1. Landing → 2. App Status
  3. Auth › Check User (NEW user) → Send OTP → Verify OTP → Signup
  3. Auth › Check User (EXISTING) → Send OTP → Login
  4. User Details (with JWT)
  5. Onboarding (BASIC → BRANDING → ACADEMIC → REVIEW + final submit)
  6. Announcements → 7. Admissions → 8. User Profile → 9. School / Drawer

Every request is annotated with a description and (where applicable) a Tests script that chains state via env vars.

exec: // Auto-default the phone number if the user forgot to set it.
console.log('[VP] phone_number was empty — defaulted to +919999900001. Set yours in the env!');

request.description: Quick sanity check that the server is running. Expect a plain-text greeting like 'Ktor: Hello, JVM — VidyaPrayag API v1 is live'.

Fetch CMS strings powering the landing/splash screen. Returns `success: true` and a `data` object with `top_tagline`, `parent_info`, `school_info`, `list_of_portals`, etc.  These are seeded by CmsSeed on first cold boot (if APP_SEED_CMS=true).
Splash-screen handshake. Returns version_check + maintenance + feature flags JSON from app_config.

item.name: 3. Auth (public)
item.description: Phone-OTP-first signup/login + email-password fallback. After /verify-otp succeeds, the row in auth_otps is marked verified and /signup or /login will accept this identifier without re-prompting.

request.description: Lookup whether a user exists for this phone/email. Response.data.is_new_user drives whether the client should call /signup or /login next.

exec: console.log('[Auth] dev_code not in response — check the server stdout for [MOCK-OTP] log line and set dev_otp manually.');

request.description:
Generates a fresh 6-digit OTP, persists in auth_otps, dispatches via the configured provider (mock prints to server stdout).

In dev (OTP_DEV_RETURN_CODE=true) the plain code is returned in `data.dev_code`. The test script captures it into `{{dev_otp}}` so the next request can use it.

Industrial features active on this endpoint:
  • UPSERT on (identifier, purpose) — resend overwrites the same row
  • 5 sends/hour rate limit → HTTP 429 OTP_RATE_LIMITED
  • Hash + salt + pepper persisted, plaintext never stored
  • Audit trail: ip_address, user_agent, device_id captured

Use `purpose=signup` instead of `login` if you want to keep signup OTPs separate.

exec: pm.test('verified', () => b.success === true && b.data && b.data.verified === true);

request.description:
Verifies the OTP. On success, marks auth_otps.is_verified=true so /signup or /login will accept this identifier.

Failure modes (each returns a distinct HTTP code + errorCode so the UI can show precise UX):
  • 404 OTP_NOT_FOUND — never sent, or already swept
  • 410 OTP_EXPIRED — 10-minute window elapsed
  • 423 OTP_LOCKED — 5 wrong attempts; row locked
  • 401 OTP_INVALID — wrong code, returns `attempts_left`

item.name: Verify OTP — WRONG CODE (test brute-force lock)

request.description:
Hit this 5 times in a row to confirm the brute-force lock kicks in.

Expected sequence (HTTP 401 each time):
  1st wrong  → 'Incorrect OTP. Attempts left: 4'
  2nd        → 'Attempts left: 3'
  3rd        → 'Attempts left: 2'
  4th        → 'Attempts left: 1'
  5th        → HTTP 423 'OTP locked — request a new one'

Then call Send OTP again — it resets attempt_count and is_locked to 0/false (UPSERT behaviour).

Creates a new app_users row.

PHONE SIGNUP requires a verified OTP for this identifier first — call Send OTP + Verify OTP before this.

EMAIL SIGNUP (use an email in `identifier` instead) requires a `password` field; OTP is optional.

On 201 the test script stores token/refresh_token/user_id/role into env vars for all subsequent authenticated requests.

exec: console.log('[Auth] Logged in:', b.data.user_id, '/', b.data.role, '| profile_completed=', b.data.profile_completed);

request.description:
Phone login. Requires a fresh OTP (call Send OTP first to refresh {{dev_otp}}).

For email login, swap `identifier` to an email and use `password` instead of `otp`:
```
{ "identifier": "abc@test.com", "password": "…" }
```

On success, JWT + refresh token are persisted to env.
Exchange a refresh token (issued by Signup/Login) for a fresh access token. Refresh tokens are persisted as SHA-256 hashes in user_sessions; revoking a session invalidates them server-side.

Returns personal_details (from app_users) + onboarding_details (step status computed from real schools/school_classes data). menu_features driven by app_config.flags JSON.

If the user has no school yet, onboarding_status=NOT_STARTED and all steps are PENDING/LOCKED.

item.name: 5. Onboarding (jwt)
item.description: BASIC → BRANDING → ACADEMIC → REVIEW. The final REVIEW submission with is_final_submission=true creates a real `schools` row, sets app_users.school_id, and stamps onboarded_at=NOW().

request.description: Returns the list of fields to render for the BASIC step + any draft values already saved for this user.

body.raw:
{
  "ob_step_type": "BASIC",
  "is_final_submission": false,
  "data_payload": {
    "school_name": "Sunrise Public School",
    "board": "CBSE",
    "medium": "English",
    "school_gender": "co_ed",
    "contact_email": "admin@sunrise.test",
    "contact_phone": "+919876500001",
    "city": "Kanpur",
    "district": "Kanpur Nagar",
    "state": "Uttar Pradesh",
    "pincode": "208001",
    "full_address": "12 Civil Lines, Kanpur"
  }
}

request.description: Upserts each (key,value) into school_onboarding_drafts for this user. No school row is created yet — that happens on REVIEW with is_final_submission=true.

Returns the list of active school_classes rows for the user's school. Empty until you insert rows (manual SQL — see LOCAL_SETUP_GUIDE Phase 9) or until you complete REVIEW (which doesn't auto-create classes either — you must INSERT them yourself).

item.name: Step REVIEW — FINAL submit (creates school row)

exec: pm.test('onboarding complete', () => b.success && b.data && b.data.is_onboarding_complete === true);
console.log('[Onboarding] Done. Re-run user/details to see updated state.');

request.description:
When is_final_submission=true on the REVIEW step, the server:
  1. Reads BASIC + BRANDING drafts for this user
  2. Creates a new `schools` row (slug = slugify(name) + first 6 chars of UUID)
  3. Sets app_users.school_id to that new UUID
  4. Sets app_users.role='school_admin' and profile_completed=true
  5. Stamps schools.onboarded_at = NOW()

After this, GET /user/details returns onboarding_status: COMPLETED.
Returns subjects for one class. Requires you to have inserted rows in school_classes + school_subjects (see LOCAL_SETUP_GUIDE Phase 9).
School scope inferred from app_users.school_id. Super-admins can pass ?school_id=UUID.

body.raw:
{
  "type": "PTM",
  "title": "Parent-Teacher Meeting — Grade 10",
  "sub_title": "Term 1 Performance Review",
  "description": "First PTM of the term. Please bring the report book.",
  "event_image": "https://cdn.vidyaprayag.com/test/ptm.jpg",
  "date": "2026-06-15"
}

request.description: Marks selected announcements as synced_to_wa=true and inserts one whatsapp_logs row per (announcement × parent_phone). Returns a fake job_id + ETA. Drop in real WhatsApp client behind this endpoint when ready.

body.raw:
{
  "student_name": "Riya Khanna",
  "parent_name": "Ankit Khanna",
  "parent_phone": "+919876500100",
  "parent_email": "ankit.k@test.com",
  "class": "10",
  "source": "website",
  "notes": "Strong in mathematics. Looking for sports facilities."
}

request.description: Allowed values: new | followup | converted | rejected. When status=converted, the server also stamps converted_at=NOW().
Delete-then-insert sync. The request body holds the FULL desired list; the server replaces all VIDEO rows for this school.

Delete-then-insert sync. Returns updated storage_used / total_storage computed from school_media rows.

Until binary upload is wired in, size_bytes per row is 0 and the MVP fallback estimate (200 KB per image) is used.
Returns is_available=false + expected_release. Not behind auth.
Requires academic_calendar rows for the user's school. Use Phase 9 of LOCAL_SETUP_GUIDE.md to insert sample events.
Joins students (filtered by grade & school) with today's attendance_records.  If a student has no row in attendance_records, they show as 'absent' by default.

item.name: 10. Parent Ecosystem (parent_api_spec)
item.description: Endpoints from parent_api_spec.artifact.md.  All UI strings/stats are backend-driven via app_config — operators can edit CMS keys in Supabase without redeploys.  All routes except /content/support require JWT (use the Login from section 3 to populate {{token}}).

request.description: Drawer & Legal screen support config.  Backed by app_config["parent_support_config"]; falls back to hard-coded defaults if not seeded.
Step 1: dropdown reference data + screen header copy.  CMS keys: parent_onboarding_step1_screen_config, parent_available_grades, parent_available_interests, parent_onboarding_footer_text.
Step 1 submit.  Creates a row in `children` for the authenticated parent.  Returns 201 with child_id; the test script auto-saves it to {{child_id}}.
Step 2: search filters (boards, focus areas, budget range).  CMS keys: parent_available_boards, parent_available_focus_areas, parent_budget_config.

Home screen handshake.  Pulls first active child, OVERDUE fee total, info alerts (CMS), top 5 active schools, and curation_logic string.  Returns child_summary=null when the parent has not onboarded a child yet.

Holistic Growth screen.  Hero band uses children.overall_progress + current_level.  Badges, NEP competencies, EI metrics, play & discovery cards all come from CMS (app_config parent_track_* keys) so ops can edit copy without redeploys.

Fees screen.  Aggregates rows in fee_records for the JWT-authenticated parent: total_collected = SUM(amount WHERE status='PAID'); outstanding = SUM(amount WHERE status IN ('DUE','OVERDUE')); overdue_count = COUNT(status='OVERDUE').  Empty state returns 0/0/$0/0%.

item.name: 11. School Ecosystem (school_api_spec)
item.description:
All endpoints from school_api_spec.artifact.md — 16 endpoints across 6 modules.
Auth: every request requires Authorization: Bearer {{token}} (inherited from the collection root).

request.description:
Returns welcome + onboarding_progress + support blocks for the school admin home.
Spec: school_api_spec.artifact.md §School Dashboard.
All strings are CMS-driven (app_config keys: school_dashboard_welcome / _steps / _support).
completed_steps is computed live from school_onboarding_drafts.

performance_trend + cards (CMS template; card[0].value is overlaid by live avg attendance %) + insights.
CMS keys: school_analytics_overview, school_analytics_cards_template, school_analytics_insights.
Returns the school_class_performance CMS blob with summary.active_students overlaid by a live count from `students` (school-scoped; optionally filtered by ?class=).
Returns the school_teacher_performance CMS blob with star_faculty overlaid by the top-3 active rows in `faculty` for the school.
studentCode accepts either the UUID primary key or the human-readable student_code (matches Results screen). KPI attendance is live across the last 30 days. Returns 404 when not found.
Pure CMS — returns the school_syllabus_coverage app_config blob verbatim.

Lists leave requests with type=student|teacher and optional status filter.
Response includes server-formatted date_range plus live approval_rate (30d) and weekly_count (7d).
Create a new pending leave request.  Returns 201 with id; the test script auto-saves it to {{leave_request_id}}.
Status must be one of Pending|Approved|Rejected (case-insensitive). Stamps actioned_by + actioned_at.

Returns active_event (next upcoming PTM; falls back to latest past row), history (last 10 past PTMs), and class_progress (live ptm_class_progress rows for the active event with computed progress = met/total).
Schedules a new PTM (counters start at 0). The test script saves the returned id to {{ptm_event_id}}.
Owner-scoped thread list (owner_user_id = JWT.sub). Server-formatted time field ('10:45 AM' for today / 'Yesterday' / 'MMM dd' older).

item.name: POST /school/messages — send (creates thread on the fly)

request.description:
When thread_id is null we create a brand-new thread (recipient defaults to self for system alerts).
Returns { thread_id, message_id } — the test script saves thread_id to {{thread_id}}.

body.raw:
{
  "thread_id": null,
  "sender_name": "Admin Desk",
  "sender_role": "Support",
  "recipient_user_id": null,
  "body": "Welcome to the admin inbox — system message."
}

request.description: Zeroes unread_count and sets is_read=true for the thread (must be owned by the authenticated user).

Returns CMS-driven available_* filter lists plus the matching slice of exam_results.
summary aggregates class_average (mean of numeric scores) and counts by status.
Portable bulk upsert against (school, test, class, subject, student_id).  Idempotent — calling it twice updates rather than duplicates.  Returns { upserted: <int> }.
